chunk_summary: Guidelines for catch-up weight gain in children with faltering growth, including explicit targets, monitoring intervals, and tapering strategies to ensure safe and effective nutritional rehabilitation.
learning_objectives: Understand the recommended weight gain targets for catch-up growth in children across different age groups.
Learn how to structure and monitor a catch-up growth plan, including time gates and response pathways.
Recognize the importance of tapering high-calorie interventions to prevent overshoot and ensure balanced growth.
key_facts: Catch-up growth is defined as weight gain at 2–3 times the expected rate for age.
Expected weight gain rates: 0–3 months: 26–31 g/day, 3–6 months: 17–18 g/day, 6–9 months: 12–13 g/day, 9–12 months: 9 g/day, beyond 12 months: 7–9 g/day.
For children over 2 years, normal weight gain is slow, typically a few kilograms per year.
At 2 weeks, success is stabilization and upward weight movement; at 4 weeks, success is a sustained trend toward the prior centile/Z score channel.
High-calorie add-ons should be tapered once the growth trajectory returns to the prior channel to prevent overshoot.
Weight monitoring should occur every 1–2 weeks initially, then every 2–4 weeks, avoiding daily weights outside neonatal scenarios.
reference_quote: Define catch-up growth as weight gain at roughly 2–3 times the expected rate for age, provided feeding is safe and linear growth is monitored so weight gain is not fat-only. (Source: NICE NG75)
